cause: spec-judge round 1 failed the builder lens on three underdetermination defects in the CR's own new scenarios: a Then asserting on a sibling-prefix path the Given never constructed, the marker literals living only in the never-frozen README so the glyphs the backfill exists to pin were unprotected, and the human-vs-structured boundary pinned at the --format json flag rather than at the surface (which the corpus's own owed TOON default would have hollowed out). All three fixed in the scenarios plus swept through the README prose, which stated the same flag-pinned boundary.

spec-judge round 2 verified all three round-1 fixes real and passed the builder lens, but failed the architect lens on a REGRESSION the producer introduced while taking round 1's optional band-placement nit: moving the render band below the outside-a-multiplexer scenario left it positionally heading the four downstream removal-ordering scenarios, filing workspace-release ordering under a listing-render heading. Cleared with the judge's own prescribed remedy, one additive section comment above the removal band. Recorded as a regression rather than glossed; the loop was judged converging (builder fail then pass, oracle pass both rounds) and the remedy comment-only, so a re-plan was not taken.
